description: Agent-readable manifest of all SPARTA Explorer components. Read this FIRST before editing any component.

rules: NEVER use raw hex colors — always reference EMBRY.* tokens
NEVER use raw font sizes — use label (10px), heading (14px), body (13px) presets
All borders use EMBRY.border, never custom rgba values
Card surfaces use card preset, darker wells use panel preset
Framework-specific colors MUST come from EMBRY.fw[framework]
Status colors follow NVIS: green=nominal, red=critical, amber=warning, blue=info

components.name: ThreatMap
components.description: ATT&CK Navigator-style grid: tactics as columns, techniques as cells with coverage indicators

props.tactics: string[] — tactic names for column headers
props.techniques: ThreatTechnique[] — technique objects with id, name, tactic, coverage, issueCount, frameworks

components.data_source: ArangoDB: sparta_techniques collection, grouped by tactic
components.composition: Used in Composed Explorer pane 1 (Threat Overview, 30% width)

components.name: ControlTable
components.description: Sortable, filterable table of security controls with framework badges and issue indicators

props.controls: ControlRow[] — control objects with id, framework, name, tactic, urlCount, relCount, knowledgeChunks, issueCount
props.onSelect: (control: ControlRow) => void — optional row click handler

features: column sorting (id, framework, name, issueCount, relCount)
components.composition: Used in Composed Explorer pane 2 (Evidence Hub, 35% width)

components.name: ChatWell
components.description: Shared SPARTA compliance chat surface with Kimi v3-style inline Evidence Case card, answer divider, shield agent identity, PDF evidence upload, and message history

props.messages: ChatMessage[] — message objects with id, role, content, type (natural|aql), timestamp, resultCount
props.onSend: (query: string, type: 'natural' | 'aql') => void — optional send handler

features: collapsed Evidence Case before final answer
PDF evidence attachment and validation state
components.data_source: ArangoDB: queries via /api/memory/* proxy (natural language → AQL translation)
components.composition: Used in SPARTA Explorer ChatTab and shared chat surfaces; Evidence Case card is implemented in src/components/shared-chat/InlineEvidenceCase.tsx

components.name: LemmaGraph
components.description: SVG knowledge graph showing cross-framework relationships with validation status

props.nodes: GraphNode[] — node objects with id, label, framework, size
props.edges: GraphEdge[] — edge objects with source, target, method, validated
props.onNodeClick: (node: GraphNode) => void — optional node click handler

components.layout: D3 force-directed simulation with drag, collision, charge repulsion
components.composition: Used in Composed Explorer pane 2 (Evidence Hub, below ControlTable)

components.name: IntegrityCard
components.description: Graph integrity status card: NOMINAL/DEGRADED/CRITICAL with coverage % and issue count
components.composition: Used in Composed Explorer pane 3 (Query & Synthesis, above ChatWell)

components.name: ControlDetail
components.description: Drill-down panel for a single control: metadata grid, weaknesses, related controls with framework badges

props.control: ControlDetailData — id, framework, name, description, controlType, domain, parentId, scope, weaknesses, relatedControls

components.data_source: ArangoDB: single control document + graph traversal for relationships
components.composition: Replaces ControlTable in Composed Explorer pane 2 when a control is selected

components.name: InspectorPanel
components.description: LemmaGraph node inspector: typed parameter editor (bool toggle, float slider, enum dropdown) + what-if cascade analysis via /lean4-prove

props.edges: GraphEdge[] — all graph edges (filtered to connected)

features: cascade result visualization with BROKEN/HOLDS status
components.data_source: ArangoDB: control metadata + /lean4-prove what-if JSON output
components.composition: Appears below LemmaGraph in pane 2 (Evidence Hub) when a node is clicked

components.name: StatusPill
components.description: Shared status badge with 7 variants (green/amber/red/blue/purple/neutral/flash). The ONE standard for all badges, tags, and counts across Unified Lab tabs.

props.flash: boolean — optional pulse animation for in-progress states

components.name: ClassificationTab
components.description: Live classifier training supervision: recharts training curve (F1/Loss), per-class recall bar chart, promotion table with StatusPill badges and clickable rows. Stop Training button sends WS command.

components.name: RegressionTab
components.description: Regressor model supervision: model table with staleness StatusPill badges, inline SVG residual scatter plot, hover-reveal Re-evaluate action, summary stat cards.
data_sources: useLabStore (static mock data, future: useLabStore aggregated)

components.name: CascadeTab
components.description: Wave 0.5 Coming Soon placeholder. Lock SVG icon, prerequisite checklist, StatusPill (blue: Track Wave 0.5), what's-coming feature list.

components.name: ModelHealthTab
components.description: Cross-lab model health registry using @tanstack/react-table for sortable/filterable columns. Summary stat cards (total/healthy/failing/stale). Hover reveals View in Lab link. Supports lab type filter dropdown.
data_sources: useLabStore (static mock, future: aggregated model registry API)

components.name: HackEvolveMonitor
components.description: Current-exploit $hack monitor. It renders the active exploit attempt and previous attempts from an adapter-shaped fixture; it does not render memory recall, dashboards, KPIs, run history, or fake operational state.

props.none: The component currently uses an in-file fixture shaped like the future /api/hack/evolve-runs/latest adapter contract.

features: previous exploits table scoped to the same attempts.jsonl run
source pills on every visible value
components.data_source: Fixture data shaped like /api/hack/evolve-runs/latest. Future adapter should derive currentExploit and previousExploits from attempts.jsonl, enrich promoted anomalies from anomalies.jsonl, and expose availableActions[].
components.test_status: TypeScript + CDP visual verification required after every layout change
components.composition: Registered in ComponentGallery under Hack / Composed as the two-pane current-exploit monitor for $hack evolve-campaign.

components.name: DagContractEditor
components.description: Minimal source-backed SCILLM DAG contract editor used by #scillm/dag-planner Design lens: dependency-derived DAG layers, graph/node Form and JSON views, expanded text editor, persisted draft saves, runtime evidence pane, and Run draft via scillm action.

props.graph: ExecGraph — selected source or draft DAG contract
props.status: ExecStatus — active runtime state and node_results for highlighting
props.events: ExecEvent[] — runtime event count and context
props.baseGraphHash: string — backend or local draft hash shown in graph header
props.onRunGraph: (graph: ExecGraph) => void — submits the current edited draft graph to the parent runner

components.data_source: Receives selected graph/status/events from ScillmDagPlanner, which loads /api/scillm/dag-viewer/snapshot and local draft records.
features: connected top-down DAG groups derived from depends_on when metadata groups are absent
graph-level Form editor for id, goal, cwd, and max_concurrency
safe dependency picker with cycle prevention
expanded text modal for node_goal and local command
runtime node-state highlighting and selected-node hash/artifact evidence
applied form/json edits persist through /api/scillm/dag-viewer/drafts; source edits create a local draft instead of mutating phase evidence
components.test_status: TypeScript + CDP screenshot verification required after every layout change
components.composition: Used by ScillmDagPlanner Design lens in place of the older ScillmExecGraphDebugger visual core for the current professional DAG viewer-editor iteration.

compositions.name: Explorer Layout

pattern.top_bar: ThoughtTrace pipeline: Ingest → Resolve → Map → Verify → Score with glow dots

compositions.section_headers: Colored pill (5x16px, rounded) + 11px font-black uppercase tracking-[0.2em] text-slate-400
variations: live (real ArangoDB data via useSpartaData hook)

compositions.name: Hack Evolve Monitor
compositions.description: Current-exploit $hack monitor centered on the active exploit attempt plus previous attempts from the same attempts.jsonl run.

pattern.top_bar: Active artifact path and exploit source only; no health, queue, score, or memory status chrome

data_hooks.name: useSpartaData
data_hooks.description: React hook fetching real SPARTA data from memory daemon via Express proxy
data_hooks.returns: controls, tactics, techniques, graphNodes, graphEdges, integrity, loading, error, refresh

lean4_integration.description: Inspector panel composes with /lean4-prove for what-if cascade propagation analysis

pdf_lab.name: PdfLabView
pdf_lab.description: Default PDF Lab surface presents the real-data agentic workflow and Coverage proof layer: agent scan, candidate pages, deterministic extraction, parity comparison, preset/code improvement, full extraction, human triage, candidate audit confirmation, and Nico evidence QA.
pdf_lab.labeling_surface: PdfLabLabelingPage uses the shared common/LeftPane for the candidate explorer, a compact canvas toolbar, and a static right selected-region inspector with visible document hierarchy path metadata.
pdf_lab.composition: Coverage starts with a candidate audit table showing all promoted sweep candidate pages, thumbnails, detected PDF element families, agent correction notes, ArangoDB memory keys, and pass/warn/fail state; Surgical Triage remains the focused human ambiguity deck.

artifact_contract.workflow_manifest: Browser-oriented phase/candidate/gate summary derived from real full-batch artifacts.
artifact_contract.human_triage_queue: Final ambiguity cards only; no solved pages should become human work.
artifact_contract.coverage_candidate_audit: Coverage renders the promoted candidate pages as evidence rows with thumbnails, detected families, agent notes, ArangoDB memory status, and final pass/warn/fail outcome.
artifact_contract.nico_qa: Post-triage stratified random sample that compares extracted JSON against original page/crop artifacts for Nico/persona-style compliance checks.
artifact_contract.gemini_review_bundle: Top-bar shared ReviewBundleButton calls POST /api/pdf-lab/gemini-review-bundle, writes /tmp/pdf-lab-gemini-review-bundle-latest.md, and copies the complete review/fix prompt with xclip.

pdf_lab.test_status: TypeScript + CDP visual verification required after every layout change

scillm_monitor.name: ScillmDashboard
scillm_monitor.description: LLM proxy batch dashboard with health ribbon, batch progress strip, incoming calls table, scoped search, status filters, and call trace inspector.
scillm_monitor.composition: Incoming table controls use a left-aligned diagnostic control row: search and batch scope picker, Inspect Batch, then status filters. A responsive divider separates the batch action from view-state filters when width allows; compact widths fall back to spacing only.
scillm_monitor.test_status: TypeScript + CDP visual verification required after every layout change

scillm_workspace.name: ScillmWorkspace
scillm_workspace.description: Final Site workspace for #scillm with internal product tabs. Mirrors the SPARTA Explorer pattern by keeping Monitor, Codex App Server sessions, and DAG Viewer-Planner inside one scillm route instead of splitting them into separate ux-lab projects.

tabs.purpose: Live scillm proxy calls, provider auth, jobs, model pools, and trace inspection.
Inspect Codex App Server subagent sessions as scillm call artifacts with persona/profile/runtime metadata, separated turns, files, tests, and protocol events.
Review and amend the plan-iterate DAG viewer-editor evidence graph from real scillm project artifacts.

scillm_workspace.test_status: TypeScript + browser screenshot verification required after tab or layout changes

scillm_app_server_session.name: ScillmAppServerSession
scillm_app_server_session.description: Artifact-backed Codex App Server interface for #scillm. It treats Nico as a scillm call variety alongside one-shot calls and exec workers, then renders the app-server JSON-RPC thread/start + turn/start run without terminal input simulation.

artifact_contract.fail_closed: The backend returns codex_app_server_session_unavailable or codex_app_server_session_incomplete instead of fake operational content when required evidence is missing.
artifact_contract.call_varieties: The UI names one-shot scillm call, scillm exec worker, and Codex App Server subagent as sibling call varieties; the selected evidence-backed variety is codex_app_server_subagent.
artifact_contract.runtime_contract: Visible runtime fields include backing runtime, persona, model, permission/sandbox profile, approval policy, terminal-input-simulation=false, and subagent-runner=false.
artifact_contract.conversation_trace: Each turn separates project/human prompt, optional course correction, Nico app-server response, terminal turn status, and duration.
artifact_contract.proof: Changed files, pytest log, workspace diff, artifact paths, and protocol event method counts are rendered from stored phase artifacts.

scillm_app_server_session.test_status: TypeScript + CDP screenshot verification required after every layout/data-contract change

scillm_dag_planner.name: ScillmDagPlanner
scillm_dag_planner.description: DAG Viewer-Editor tab for scillm. Loads a fail-closed snapshot adapter from /api/scillm/dag-viewer/snapshot, keeps the DagExplorerPane, and uses DagContractEditor in the Design lens for a professional source-backed connected DAG + Form/JSON selected-node editor. Source, Trace, Checkpoints, and Debug lenses remain available for lower-level inspection.

artifact_contract.fail_closed: If no complete runtime artifact set or plan-graph fallback exists for the resolved phase, the tab renders DAG evidence unavailable instead of fake graph state. Runtime artifact discovery supports both exec-run directories and current phase evidence files.
artifact_contract.phase_gate: The tab surfaces PHASE_STATUS review_comparison when phase closure is still pending.
artifact_contract.model_selection: Review fanout models come from /v1/scillm/models review_fanout_models, preserving the no text/text-* selection contract.
artifact_contract.inspectability: The DAG planner uses the scillm-dag-planner modifier so the DAG evidence surface scrolls inside the #scillm final-site panel; graph, footer events, and node inspector content must remain reachable at desktop viewport height.
artifact_contract.viewport_controls: The graph canvas exposes zoom in, zoom out, move/pan, and fit controls in the top-right canvas well and the same actions in the canvas context menu.
artifact_contract.layout_spacing: The DAG layout is top-down for editing: depth maps to vertical position, same-depth nodes fan out horizontally, and the internal graph canvas sizes from deepest layer and widest layer so adjacent nodes do not overlap 270px cards.
artifact_contract.plan_iterate_shape: Current plan-iterate graphs render as a top-down editable workflow map: synthesis/goal upstream, review fanout across a horizontal layer, aggregate/implement/verify/validate downstream.
artifact_contract.node_inspector: Node inspection uses a persistent right-side selected-node Form/JSON workbench from DagContractEditor. Form edits common contract fields and JSON remains the raw escape hatch; expanded text editing is available for long goals and commands.
artifact_contract.view_model: dagViewModel.ts derives DagViewModel from executable graph nodes, status/events/artifacts, draft amendment diff, and separate UI layout state. Build/Run/Debug are the primary product modes; Evidence/Plan edit/Nico proposals are lower-level capabilities.
artifact_contract.base_graph_hash: GET /api/scillm/dag-viewer/snapshot returns a backend-computed sha256 canonical executable graph hash. UI draft saves include that hash and display stale-hash warnings when the draft base hash no longer matches the current snapshot.
artifact_contract.amendment_drafts: Build mode semantic edits create local AmendmentDraft operations. Save Draft posts operations to /api/scillm/dag-viewer/amendments; Apply Amendment is intentionally disabled until a backend apply validator exists.
artifact_contract.dag_explorer: The left DagExplorerPane is a shared DAG workspace control for #scillm/dag-planner. It lists the source DAG plus persisted local draft DAGs, supports search, open/select, add, duplicate, close, and local draft delete. Source DAG delete remains disabled because it is backed by phase evidence, not a workspace draft.
artifact_contract.starter_dag: New project DAG creates a valid three-node local_command exec graph: define-goal writes a project-goal artifact, check-goal validates it, and final-report emits the terminal report. The first node is selected immediately so the inspector is never blank after creating or opening a draft.
artifact_contract.draft_persistence: Local draft DAGs are saved under the scillm project through /api/scillm/dag-viewer/drafts. DagContractEditor Apply/Duplicate/Delete now persists the edited graph through the parent planner. Editing an authoritative source DAG creates a new local draft and switches the explorer to that draft instead of mutating source phase evidence.
artifact_contract.run_active_dag: The Run DAG action submits the selected graph to /api/scillm/v1/scillm/exec/graph and renders returned run_id, state, and node_results as the active graph status. Test manifest test-interactions/manifests/scillm-dag-viewer-editor.yaml covers edit -> run -> inspect run evidence.
artifact_contract.build_mode_controls: Build mode exposes add child, add sibling, add gate, disable node, and archive node controls in the node inspector. These mutate only the local draft graph and formal amendment diff.
artifact_contract.five_lens_shell: The top-level DAG viewer-editor shell separates Source (raw graph/status/events JSON), Design (visual graph and amendment drafting), Trace (live/review execution chronology), Checkpoints (human gates and interrupt-style queue), and Debug (evidence, artifacts, hashes, and missing fields). This borrows product requirements from LangGraph/Studio, Agent Flow, and Agent Inbox without copying source code.
artifact_contract.design_lens: Design lens now renders DagContractEditor as the primary professional DAG viewer-editor surface: left dependency-layered DAG projection, graph/node Form and JSON views, right selected-node runtime evidence, source-backed runtime state, and current draft graph submission through /api/scillm/v1/scillm/exec/graph.

scillm_dag_planner.test_status: TypeScript + local adapter health + actual screenshot inspection required before claiming ready

scillm_dag_explorer_pane.name: DagExplorerPane
scillm_dag_explorer_pane.description: Shared left DAG explorer for scillm DAG workspaces. Provides search, open/select, add, duplicate, close, and persisted local draft delete affordances.

props.items: DagExplorerItem[] — visible source and draft DAG records with node/edge counts, active state, and delete capability.
props.onSearchChange: (value: string) => void — update the search query.
props.onAdd: () => void — create a new local draft DAG.
props.onDuplicate: (id: string) => void — clone a DAG into a local draft.
props.onClose: (id: string) => void — close a DAG from the explorer.
props.onDelete: (id: string) => void — delete a local draft DAG.

scillm_dag_explorer_pane.data_contract: Source DAGs are backed by /api/scillm/dag-viewer/snapshot. Added and duplicated DAGs are saved through /api/scillm/dag-viewer/drafts as workspace drafts and remain separate from authoritative phase evidence until a scillm amendment/apply endpoint accepts them.
scillm_dag_explorer_pane.test_status: TypeScript + CDP screenshot verification required after component changes

hack_monitor.name: HackEvolveMonitor
hack_monitor.description: Current-exploit monitor for $hack evolve-campaign. It removes dashboard theater by showing only currentExploit, previousExploits, recent events, and availableActions[] with source-backed artifact selectors.

artifact_contract.summary: Supporting run metadata only; not the primary monitor object.
artifact_contract.attempts: Primary source of truth for currentExploit and previousExploits. Each visible exploit row must resolve to an attempts.jsonl selector such as attempt_id.
artifact_contract.anomalies: Promoted anomaly evidence used to enrich currentExploit.latestResult and blocker context.
artifact_contract.generation_summaries: Strategy/lineage context only; not primary monitor content.
artifact_contract.promotion_tasks: Downstream handoff tasks generated from promoted anomalies; not exploit attempts.
artifact_contract.docker_logs: Optional evidence opened through availableActions[]; not a dashboard log wall.

known_gaps_exposed_by_ui: Define attempts.jsonl selectors and line-pointer support.
Map anomalies.jsonl promoted records onto currentExploit without inventing state.
hack_monitor.adapter_contract: GET /api/hack/evolve-runs/latest returns active artifact path, currentExploit, previousExploits, latestEvents, availableActions, and sourceDetail for every visible field.
hack_monitor.app_shell_route: Sidebar project id hack renders HackEvolveMonitor directly at #hack/final-site and appears near the top of the project list.

route_shell_contracts.contract: SPARTA Explorer final-site routes render inside the ux-lab project housing and keep the SPARTA tab strip visible, including Controls and Coverage, even when the QRAs tab is active.
route_shell_contracts.interaction_contract: Navigation buttons in the SPARTA tab strip keep stable data-qid/data-qs-action selectors and remain available from the QRA review workspace.
route_shell_contracts.qras_layout_contract: The housed QRAs route uses bounded responsive columns so the filter rail, dense QRA table, editable draft, inline CAE artifact, and fixed chat composer remain visible without horizontal clipping at a 1440px desktop viewport.

planned_components.name: QRADetail
planned_components.description: Individual Question/Response/Answer triple view with source evidence
planned_components.data_source: ArangoDB: QRA documents linked to controls

planned_components.name: DimensionCard
planned_components.data_source: ArangoDB: taxonomy tags on controls and relationships

gallery.structure: Folder tree sidebar (search + collapsible folders) → Main preview (toolbar + variation tabs + canvas)

endpoints.ALL /api/memory/*: Proxy to embry-memory daemon via Unix socket at /run/user/1000/embry/memory.sock

from_pi_design_deck.why: When components connect to real ArangoDB data, the server needs to push query results to the browser without polling. Add GET /api/events SSE endpoint.
Agent needs to tell the server 'run this AQL and show results in ChatWell' — not just proxy raw HTTP. Add POST /api/command with { command, args } contract.
Human says 'show me more variations' → agent generates them on demand. Maps to component variation generation.

from_dogpile_research.why: Agent captures screenshots after changes, compares against baselines. Catches unintended regressions.
Automated check that no component uses raw hex/px — all values via EMBRY.* tokens.
